dependencies: Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC) for Kazakhstan oil exports through Russia
risks: Transportation disruption of Kazakhstan production through CPC pipeline due to geopolitical issues
Logistics constraints or unexpected unavailability of distribution channels
Concentration risk in certain geographic regions

regulatory_risks: Changes in taxes, duties, or government royalty rates including retroactive claims and windfall profit taxes
Changes in environmental regulations affecting offshore drilling, decommissioning, water use, emissions, hydraulic fracturing, production or use of plastics
Actions to delay or deny necessary licenses and permits
Restrictions on oil and gas leases or transportation/export of products
Adoption of regulations mandating efficiency standards, emission standards, use of alternative fuels
Greenhouse gas emission regulations including cap and trade regimes, carbon taxes, carbon-based import duties
Disclosure regulations creating competitive disadvantages or requiring competitively sensitive information
Government actions to cancel contracts, redenominate currency, renounce obligations, or expropriate assets
Punitive European taxes on oil and gas sector
Climate change regulations and net-zero policies
Restrictions on foreign investment in oil and gas sector
Lack of legal certainty in some jurisdictions with undeveloped legal systems
Litigation targeting the Company or industry to reduce hydrocarbon production
Additional European taxes on the energy sector

sanction_risks: Restrictions on doing business in certain countries due to US and other jurisdiction sanctions
Russia sanctions and expropriation of Sakhalin-1 project
Potential disruption of Kazakhstan oil transportation through Russia due to military conflict
Trade sanctions and trade controls restricting business activities
Import/export restrictions based on point of origin
